message:
fix(mvcc): tighten cell-budget accounting and cap SSI history growth

Harden the new cell-level MVCC path by making its memory accounting less
optimistic and by reducing auxiliary history growth in the concurrent-begin
path.

This commit groups the MVCC changes that all address the same failure mode:
cell-level concurrency logic that looked correct in isolation but could still
overshoot memory budgets or let bookkeeping structures grow more than needed.

Included changes:
- add the missing `fsqlite-btree` lockfile edge required by the current
  `fsqlite-mvcc` dependency graph;
- lower the SSI history cap in `begin_concurrent.rs` and emit explicit status
  logging around history pressure so saturation is easier to diagnose during
  concurrent-writer runs;
- document and test the cell-routing escalation rules in `cell_routing.rs`,
  especially the requirement that callers honor explicit escalation state
  before routing work down the cell path;
- make `cell_visibility.rs` charge transactions against the actual delta
  memory footprint while holding the per-transaction tracker lock, which
  closes the prior check-then-record race where same-transaction writers could
  collectively exceed the intended budget window.

Why these files belong together:
- they all sit on the boundary between cell-level write admission and the
  concurrency bookkeeping that protects it;
- landing them separately would hide the fact that the routing tests,
  budget fix, and reduced SSI history pressure are one coherent stabilization
  pass on the same MVCC feature area.
